records.ety: [L. fumus; akin to Skr. dhūma smoke, dhū to shake, fan a flame, cf. Gr. qy`ein to sacrifice, storm, rage, qy`mon, qy`mos, thyme, and perh. to E. dust: cf. OF. fum smoke, F. fume. Cf. Dust, n., Femerell, Thyme.]

defs: Exhalation; volatile matter (esp. noxious vapor or smoke) ascending in a dense body; smoke; vapor; reek; as, the fumes of tobacco.
quotes: The fumes of new shorn hay.

defs: Rage or excitement which deprives the mind of self-control; as, the fumes of passion.
Anything vaporlike, unsubstantial, or airy; idle conceit; vain imagination.
quotes: A show of fumes and fancies.

defs: The incense of praise; inordinate flattery.
quotes: To smother him with fumes and eulogies.

defs: Solid material deposited by condensation of fumes; as, lead fume (a grayish powder chiefly lead sulphate).

records.ety: [Cf. F. fumer, L. fumare to smoke. See Fume, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Fumed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Fuming.]

defs: To smoke; to throw off fumes, as in combustion or chemical action; to rise up, as vapor.
quotes: Silenus lay, Whose constant cups lay fuming to his brain.

defs: To be as in a mist; to be dulled and stupefied.
To pass off in fumes or vapors.
quotes: Their parts are kept from fuming away by their fixity.

defs: To be in a rage; to be hot with anger.
quotes: He frets, he fumes, he stares, he stamps the ground.
While her mother did fret, and her father did fume.

defs: To expose to the action of fumes; to treat with vapors, smoke, etc.; as, to bleach straw by fuming it with sulphur; to fill with fumes, vapors, odors, etc., as a room.
quotes: She fumed the temple with an odorous flame.
They demi-deify and fume him so.

defs: To throw off in vapor, or as in the form of vapor.
quotes: The heat will fume away most of the scent.
How vicious hearts fume frenzy to the brain!
